judgments.comparative_verdict: Report 2 is stronger: it hits all four expectations including the missing env-level global gate, which Report 1 omits.

report_1.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made in `auto_send_reminders_enabled` at `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_2.evidence: Reminders **will be sent automatically**. The decision is made by `auto_send_reminders_enabled` in `backend/app/feature_flags.py:10-13`:

report_1.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:12` from `return True` to `return False` inside `auto_send_reminders_enabled`, so a clinic that has never explicitly enabled the feature does not have reminders sent on its behalf.

report_2.evidence: I would change `backend/app/feature_flags.py:11-12` so a missing/`None` value resolves to `False`

report_1.evidence: `auto_send_reminders_enabled` (added in `aa4cf8d`, SLOT-214) is a line-for-line structural copy of `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... A harmless UI default doesn't transfer to a communications-consent default just because the code shape is convenient to copy.

report_2.evidence: Commit `25509a7` ("SLOT-190 tenant settings and calendar view preference"...) introduced the *first* resolver, `compact_calendar_view_enabled`... The two flags are in different risk categories per project documentation's own framework (display preference vs. automated outbound messaging to patients)

**Check for an env-level master switch**: I grepped the whole `slotwise` tree and found none — `auto_send_reminders_enabled` is the *only* gate before real patient contact info gets a message sent... I'd want a deploy-wide kill switch independent of the per-tenant JSONB value
